title: The Miranda Hypothesis: How Hamilton Poisoned Persona Evals - Jacob E. Thomas, Results Gen
summary: Jacob E. Thomas argues that persona-eval benchmarks systematically fail to detect when character-based AI systems output culturally dominant composites rather than documentary-grounded reasoning, a failure mode he names Miranda distortion. He proposes epistemic simulation with historian-in-the-loop evaluation and describes the pre-registered Prism Experiment as a methodological solution using temporally-anchored diagnostic questions and domain expert rubrics.
writeup: Jacob E. Thomas argues that persona-eval benchmarks systematically fail to catch when character-based AI systems produce culturally dominant composites instead of documentary-grounded reasoning, a failure mode he names Miranda distortion. The talk matters because standard benchmarks and RLHF can reward fluent, popularized personas while missing whether a simulated character actually reasons from period-accurate sources. As a fix, Thomas proposes epistemic simulation with a historian in the loop and describes the pre-registered Prism Experiment, which uses temporally-anchored diagnostic questions and domain expert rubrics to evaluate agents.
